title: TEMPLATE: Name the problem in one line — e.g. 'The need is bigger than the safety net.'
body: TEMPLATE: A short problem statement (2–3 sentences). Who is affected, how many, and why it matters right now. Lead with the human stakes, not the org.
_stats_help: Each stat is one card. type = the visual treatment. You can add, remove, or reorder cards freely — the layout reflows. The example NUMBERS are left realistic so the visuals render in preview; replace them with your own. All descriptive COPY uses TEMPLATE: so the content check flags anything you forgot. Source url: paste a real link to make it a clickable citation, or set to empty "" if it's internal data with no public link.

stats.label: TEMPLATE: What this number counts — e.g. 'people on the waitlist each year'

source.text: TEMPLATE: Publisher, title, year — e.g. 'U.S. Census Bureau, ACS Table B19013, 2023'
source.url: TEMPLATE: full URL to this source (or empty "" for internal data)

stats.label: TEMPLATE: What the percentage means — e.g. 'of those who ask are turned away'

source.text: TEMPLATE: Publisher, title, year — e.g. 'National Council, Annual Survey, 2023'
source.url: TEMPLATE: full URL to this source (or empty "" for internal data)

stats.label: TEMPLATE: What the ratio describes — e.g. 'go without the service entirely'

source.text: TEMPLATE: Publisher, title, year — e.g. 'State Dept. of Health, Data Brief 12, 2022'
source.url: TEMPLATE: full URL to this source (or empty "" for internal data)

stats.label: TEMPLATE: What the bar measures — e.g. 'Available funding meets just a fraction of demand'

source.text: TEMPLATE: Publisher, title, year — e.g. 'Urban Institute, Funding Gap Report, 2023'
source.url: TEMPLATE: full URL to this source (or empty "" for internal data)

stats.label: TEMPLATE: What rose or fell — e.g. 'increase in demand since 2020'

source.text: TEMPLATE: Publisher, title, year — e.g. 'Internal intake data, 2020–2024'
source.url: TEMPLATE: full URL to this source (or empty "" for internal data)

stats.text: TEMPLATE: A single sentence that lands the scale of the problem and sets up why the current system can't close the gap — e.g. 'who need this support actually receive it, and the gap widens every year.'

source.text: TEMPLATE: Publisher, title, year — e.g. 'SAMHSA, National Findings, 2022'
source.url: TEMPLATE: full URL to this source (or empty "" for internal data)

stats.eyebrow: What changes when we step in
stats.text: TEMPLATE: The outcome your model produces — the single number you move and track. e.g. 'who get coordinated support early stay stable a year later.'
stats.benchmark: TEMPLATE: What that number is measured against, so it has meaning — e.g. 'vs. 3 in 10 without coordinated support'
stats.method: TEMPLATE: Based on [method, e.g. 12-month follow-up surveys] of [N] participants · [Year]

source.text: TEMPLATE: Your own outcome data — e.g. 'Internal program evaluation, 2024'
source.url: TEMPLATE: link to your published outcomes report (or empty "" for internal data)

humanAnchor.quote: TEMPLATE: A direct-voice quote from someone you serve — e.g. "I've been on the waitlist for six months and I still don't know where to turn."

gapsTitle: TEMPLATE: Why it persists — e.g. 'The system has gaps that keep people stuck'
_gaps_help: Add or remove gap items freely — the grid reflows to fit 2, 3, or 4. Keep them at the same altitude as your turn-chain below (diagnose a system → prescribe for the system).

gaps.heading: TEMPLATE: First gap — e.g. 'No coordinated intake'
gaps.body: TEMPLATE: One sentence on what this gap is and the consequence it creates for the people affected.

gaps.heading: TEMPLATE: Second gap — e.g. 'Funding follows crisis, not prevention'
gaps.body: TEMPLATE: One sentence on what this gap is and the consequence it creates.

gaps.heading: TEMPLATE: Third gap — e.g. 'No one follows up after the first contact'
gaps.body: TEMPLATE: One sentence on what this gap is and the consequence it creates.

turn.line: TEMPLATE: The sentence where your org enters — e.g. 'That is the gap we were built to start closing.'
turn._chain_help: If → Then → Impact: the condition you create, the change that follows, the measured result. Keep all three at SYSTEMS altitude to match your gaps above.

chain.text: TEMPLATE: The structural thing you build — e.g. 'we build the coordinated intake the system is missing,'
TEMPLATE: The system-level change — e.g. 'the gap closes for everyone who comes after, not only the people we serve directly,'
TEMPLATE: The measured shift in the system — e.g. 'and the system itself gets better at catching people early, year over year.'

turn.coda: TEMPLATE: One honest sentence on what this org can't do alone — e.g. 'We are one part of the response, not the whole answer.'

reviewedDate: TEMPLATE: When this section's data was last reviewed — e.g. 'March 2025'
